The rule sending a based space (рқ‘Ӣ, рқ‘Ҙ0 ) to its fundamental group рқңӢ1 (рқ‘Ӣ, рқ‘Ҙ0 ) and a based map в„Һ вҲ¶ (рқ‘Ӣ, рқ‘Ҙ0 ) вҶ’ (рқ‘Ң , рқ‘Ұ0 ) to its induced homomorphism в„ҺвҲ— вҲ¶ рқңӢ1 (рқ‘Ӣ, рқ‘Ҙ0 ) вҶ’ рқңӢ1 (рқ‘Ң , рқ‘Ұ0 ) is a functor from the category of based spaces and based maps to the category of groups and (group) homomorphisms.

Proof. Assume that рқ‘“ вҲ¶ рқҗј вҶ’ рқ‘Ӣ is a loop in рқ‘Ӣ based at рқ‘Ҙ0 . Then

(в„Һ2 в—Ұв„Һ1 )вҲ— ([рқ‘“ ]) = [(в„Һ2 в—Ұв„Һ1 )в—Ұрқ‘“ ]

and ( ) (в„Һ2 )вҲ— (в„Һ1 )вҲ— ([рқ‘“ ]) = [в„Һ2 в—Ұ(в„Һ1 в—Ұрқ‘“ )].

Since composition of continuous maps is associative, i.e., (в„Һ2 в—Ұв„Һ1 )в—Ұрқ‘“ = в„Һ2 в—Ұ(в„Һ1 в—Ұрқ‘“ ), these path homo- topy classes must be equal. Hence, (в„Һ2 в—Ұв„Һ1 )вҲ— = (в„Һ2 )вҲ— в—Ұ(в„Һ1 )вҲ— . If idрқ‘Ӣ вҲ¶ рқ‘Ӣ вҶ’ рқ‘Ӣ is the identity map, then idрқ‘Ӣ в—Ұрқ‘“ = рқ‘“ , and so, (idрқ‘Ӣ )вҲ— ([рқ‘“ ]) = [idрқ‘Ӣ в—Ұрқ‘“ ] = [рқ‘“ ]. Hence, (idрқ‘Ӣ )вҲ— is the identity automorphism of рқңӢ1 (рқ‘Ӣ, рқ‘Ҙ0 ).

The following corollary of Theorem 7.24 tells us that the fundamental group is a topological in- variant of based spaces.

nodes.title: Corollary 7.25
nodes.originalText:
Let (рқ‘Ӣ, рқ‘Ҙ0 ) and (рқ‘Ң , рқ‘Ұ0 ) be based spaces. If в„Һ вҲ¶ рқ‘Ӣ вҶ’ рқ‘Ң is a homeomorphism such that в„Һ(рқ‘Ҙ0 ) = рқ‘Ұ0 , then в„ҺвҲ— вҲ¶ рқңӢ1 (рқ‘Ӣ, рқ‘Ҙ0 ) вҶ’ рқңӢ1 (рқ‘Ң , рқ‘Ұ0 ) is an isomorphism.

The fundamental group thus allows us to distinguish between two based spaces provided that their funda- mental groups are not isomorphic. By Example 7.17, we know that рқңӢ1 (в„қ, 0) = 0 and рқңӢ1 (в„қ2 , 0) = 0. However, by Example 6.16, we know that в„қ and в„қ2 are not homeomorphic. Hence, it is possible for two non-homeomorphic based spaces to have isomorphic fundamental groups.

Proof. We must show that в„ҺвҲ— is bijective. Let рқ‘ҳ вҲ¶ рқ‘Ң вҶ’ рқ‘Ӣ be the inverse homemorphism of в„Һ such that рқ‘ҳ(рқ‘Ұ0 ) = рқ‘Ҙ0 . Then, by Theorem 7.24, рқ‘ҳвҲ— в—Ұв„ҺвҲ— = (рқ‘ҳв—Ұв„Һ)вҲ— = (idрқ‘Ӣ )вҲ— , and в„ҺвҲ— в—Ұрқ‘ҳвҲ— = (в„Һв—Ұрқ‘ҳ)вҲ— = (idрқ‘Ң )вҲ— . Hence, рқ‘ҳвҲ— = (в„ҺвҲ— )вҲ’1 .

To compute the fundamental group of a product space, we may use the following theorem.

nodes.title: Theorem 7.26
nodes.originalText:
Let (рқ‘Ӣ, рқ‘Ҙ0 ) and (рқ‘Ң , рқ‘Ұ0 ) be based spaces. Then рқңӢ1 (рқ‘ӢГ—рқ‘Ң , (рқ‘Ҙ0 , рқ‘Ұ0 )) is isomorphic to the direct product рқңӢ1 (рқ‘Ӣ, рқ‘Ҙ0 ) Г— рқңӢ1 (рқ‘Ң , рқ‘Ұ0 ).

7.3 Homotopy type We will see in this section how we may obtain information about the fundamental group by way of homotopy types. This allows us to compute the fundamental group of a topological space by computing the fundamental group of some other topological space that is hopefully easier to work with.

nodes.title: Lemma 7.27
nodes.originalText:
Let (рқ‘Ӣ, рқ‘Ҙ0 ) and (рқ‘Ң , рқ‘Ұ0 ) be based spaces, and let в„Һ вҲ¶ (рқ‘Ӣ, рқ‘Ҙ0 ) вҶ’ (рқ‘Ң , рқ‘Ұ0 ) and рқ‘ҳ вҲ¶ (рқ‘Ӣ, рқ‘Ҙ0 ) вҶ’ (рқ‘Ң , рқ‘Ұ0 ) be based maps. nodes.title: Retractions
nodes.originalText: Let рқ‘Ӣ be a topological space, and let рқҗҙ be a subspace of рқ‘Ӣ. We say that a continuous map рқ‘ҹ вҲ¶ рқ‘Ӣ вҶ’ рқҗҙ is a retraction of рқ‘Ӣ onto рқҗҙ if рқ‘ҹ(рқ‘Һ) = рқ‘Һ for each рқ‘Һ вҲҲ рқҗҙ. If there is a retraction of рқ‘Ӣ onto рқҗҙ, we say that рқҗҙ is a retract of рқ‘Ӣ.

nodes.title: Example 7.29
nodes.originalText:
The circle, рқ‘Ҷ 1 , is a retract of в„қ2 в§ө {0} where в„қ2 is given the standard topology. Specifically, the continuous map рқ‘ҹ вҲ¶ в„қ2 в§ө {0} вҶ’ рқ‘Ҷ 1 given by рқ‘Ҙ рқ‘ҹ(рқ‘Ҙ) = вҖ–рқ‘ҘвҖ– вҲҡ where вҖ–рқ‘ҘвҖ– = рқ‘Ҙ21 + рқ‘Ҙ22 , is a retraction of в„қ2 в§ө {0} onto рқ‘Ҷ 1 .

nodes.title: Lemma 7.30
nodes.originalText:
Let рқ‘Ӣ be a topological space, and let рқҗҙ be a subspace of рқ‘Ӣ. If рқ‘Ҙ0 вҲҲ рқҗҙ and рқҗҙ is a retract of рқ‘Ӣ, then the homomorphism рқ‘–вҲ— вҲ¶ рқңӢ1 (рқҗҙ, рқ‘Ҙ0 ) вҶ’ рқңӢ1 (рқ‘Ӣ, рқ‘Ҙ0 ) induced by the inclusion map рқ‘– вҲ¶ рқҗҙ вҶ’ рқ‘Ӣ is a monomorphism.

Proof. Let рқ‘ҹ вҲ¶ рқ‘Ӣ вҶ’ рқҗҙ be a retraction of рқ‘Ӣ onto рқҗҙ. Then, by Theorem 7.24 and the definition of retraction, we have the following commutative diagram рқ‘–вҲ— рқңӢ1 (рқҗҙ, рқ‘Ҙ0 ) рқңӢ1 (рқ‘Ӣ, рқ‘Ҙ0 ) рқ‘ҹвҲ— (idрқҗҙ )вҲ— рқңӢ1 (рқҗҙ, рқ‘Ҙ0 )

where idрқҗҙ is the identity map of рқҗҙ. By Theorem 7.24, we know that (idрқҗҙ )вҲ— is an isomorphism. Thus рқ‘ҹвҲ— в—Ұрқ‘–вҲ— is an isomorphism, and hence, also bijective. This implies that рқ‘–вҲ— is injective. Hence, рқ‘–вҲ— is a monomorphism.

The homomorphism induced by the inclusion of рқҗҙ into рқ‘Ӣ is an isomorphism if рқҗҙ is a deformation retract.

76 7.3. Homotopy type

nodes.title: Deformation retracts
nodes.originalText:
Let рқ‘Ӣ be a topological space, and let рқҗҙ be a subspace of рқ‘Ӣ. A homotopy рқҗ»вҲ¶ рқ‘Ӣ Г—рқҗј вҶ’ рқ‘Ӣ is called a deformation retraction of рқ‘Ӣ onto рқҗҙ if рқҗ»(рқ‘Ҙ, 0) = рқ‘Ҙ and рқҗ»(рқ‘Ҙ, 1) вҲҲ рқҗҙ for all рқ‘Ҙ вҲҲ рқ‘Ӣ, and рқҗ»(рқ‘Һ, рқ‘Ў) = рқ‘Һ for all рқ‘Һ вҲҲ рқҗҙ and all рқ‘Ў вҲҲ рқҗј. We say that рқҗҙ is a deformation retract of рқ‘Ӣ.

The map рқ‘ҹ вҲ¶ рқ‘Ӣ вҶ’ рқҗҙ defined by рқ‘ҹ(рқ‘Ҙ) = рқҗ»(рқ‘Ҙ, 1) is a retraction of рқ‘Ӣ onto рқҗҙ. Thus рқҗ» is a homotopy between the identity map of рқ‘Ӣ and the map рқ‘–в—Ұрқ‘ҹ where рқ‘– вҲ¶ рқҗҙ вҶ’ рқ‘Ӣ is the inclusion map. Note that some authors call what we have defined as a deformation retract, a strong deformation retract. In this terminology a homotopy рқҗ» вҲ¶ рқ‘Ӣ Г— рқҗј вҶ’ рқ‘Ң is a deformation retract of рқ‘Ӣ onto рқҗҙ if рқҗ»(рқ‘Ҙ, 0) = рқ‘Ҙ and рқҗ»(рқ‘Ҙ, 1) вҲҲ рқҗҙ for all рқ‘Ҙ вҲҲ рқ‘Ӣ, and рқҗ»(рқ‘Һ, 1) = рқ‘Һ for all рқ‘Һ вҲҲ рқҗҙ.

Every deformation retract is also a retract but the converse is not true in general.

nodes.title: Example 7.32
nodes.originalText: Let рқ‘Ӣ be a topological space, and let рқ‘Һ вҲҲ рқ‘Ӣ. Then рқҗҙ = {рқ‘Һ} is retract of рқ‘Ӣ with retraction рқ‘ҹ вҲ¶ рқ‘Ӣ вҶ’ рқҗҙ given by рқ‘ҹ(рқ‘Ҙ) = рқ‘Һ. If рқҗҙ is a deformation retract of рқ‘Ӣ, then рқ‘Ӣ must be path-connected: the deformation retraction gives that there is a path from each рқ‘Ҙ вҲҲ рқ‘Ӣ to рқ‘Һ. However, there are path-connected spaces that do not deformation retract onto a point.

nodes.title: Theorem 7.33
nodes.originalText:
Let рқ‘Ӣ be a topological space, and let рқҗҙ be a subspace of рқ‘Ӣ. If рқ‘Ҙ0 вҲҲ рқҗҙ and рқҗҙ is a deformation retract of рқ‘Ӣ, then the homomorphism рқ‘–вҲ— вҲ¶ рқңӢ1 (рқҗҙ, рқ‘Ҙ0 ) вҶ’ рқңӢ1 (рқ‘Ӣ, рқ‘Ҙ0 ) induced by the inclusion map рқ‘– вҲ¶ рқҗҙ вҶ’ рқ‘Ӣ is an isomorphism.

Proof. Let рқҗ» вҲ¶ рқ‘Ӣ Г— рқҗј вҶ’ рқ‘Ӣ be a deformation retraction of рқ‘Ӣ onto рқҗҙ, and let a retraction рқ‘ҹ вҲ¶ рқ‘Ӣ вҶ’ рқҗҙ of рқ‘Ӣ onto рқҗҙ be given by рқ‘ҹ(рқ‘Ҙ) = рқҗ»(рқ‘Ҙ, 1). By Lemma 7.27 and Theorem 7.24, we have рқ‘–вҲ— в—Ұрқ‘ҹвҲ— = (idрқ‘Ӣ )вҲ— where idрқ‘Ӣ is the identity map of рқ‘Ӣ. Since рқ‘ҹв—Ұрқ‘– = idрқҗҙ , we have рқ‘ҹвҲ— в—Ұрқ‘–вҲ— = (idрқҗҙ )вҲ— where idрқҗҙ is the identity map of рқҗҙ. Thus both рқ‘ҹвҲ— в—Ұрқ‘–вҲ— and рқ‘–вҲ— в—Ұрқ‘ҹвҲ— are isomorphisms. Hence, рқ‘–вҲ— вҲ¶ рқңӢ1 (рқҗҙ, рқ‘Ҙ0 ) вҶ’ рқңӢ1 (рқ‘Ӣ, рқ‘Ҙ0 ) is an isomorphism with рқ‘ҹвҲ— as its inverse.

We may generalize the notion of deformation retraction. This leads us to the notion of homotopy equivalence.

nodes.title: Homotopy equivalences
nodes.originalText:
Let рқ‘Ӣ and рқ‘Ң be topological spaces. If рқ‘“ вҲ¶ рқ‘Ӣ вҶ’ рқ‘Ң and рқ‘” вҲ¶ рқ‘Ң вҶ’ рқ‘Ӣ are continuous maps such that рқ‘”в—Ұрқ‘“ is homotopic to the identity map of рқ‘Ӣ, idрқ‘Ӣ , and рқ‘“ в—Ұрқ‘” is homotopic to the identity map of рқ‘Ң , idрқ‘Ң , we say that рқ‘“ and рқ‘” are homotopy equivalences. We say that each of рқ‘“ and рқ‘” is a homotopy inverse of the other.

Clearly, every homeomorphism is also a homotopy equivalence. The converse is not true in general.

nodes.title: Example 7.35
nodes.originalText:
Let в„қ be the set of real numbers equipped with the standard topology. Then for any рқ‘қ вҲҲ в„қ, the map рқ‘“ вҲ¶ в„қ вҶ’ {рқ‘қ} given by рқ‘“ (рқ‘Ҙ) = рқ‘қ is a homotopy equivalence with homotopy inverse рқ‘” вҲ¶ {рқ‘қ} вҶ’ в„қ given by рқ‘”(рқ‘қ) = рқ‘қ. It is clear that рқ‘“ is not a homeomorphism. In fact, there are no homeomorphisms between в„қ and {рқ‘қ}.

Chapter 7. The fundamental group 77

nodes.title: Homotopy types
nodes.originalText:
Let рқ‘Ӣ and рқ‘Ң be topological spaces. We say that рқ‘Ӣ and рқ‘Ң have the same homotopy type if there is a homotopy equivalence рқ‘“ вҲ¶ рқ‘Ӣ вҶ’ рқ‘Ң .

It is common to also refer to topological spaces of the same homotopy type as homotopy equivalent. We end this section with a result showing that topological spaces of the same homotopy type have isomorphic fundamental groups. To prove the result we will need the following lemma.

nodes.title: Lemma 7.37
nodes.originalText:
Let рқ‘Ӣ and рқ‘Ң be topological spaces, and let рқ‘“ вҲ¶ рқ‘Ӣ вҶ’ рқ‘Ң and рқ‘” вҲ¶ рқ‘Ӣ вҶ’ рқ‘Ң be continuous maps such that рқ‘“ (рқ‘Ҙ0 ) = рқ‘Ұ0 and рқ‘”(рқ‘Ҙ0 ) = рқ‘Ұ1 . If рқҗ» вҲ¶ рқ‘Ӣ Г— рқҗј вҶ’ рқ‘Ң is a homotopy from рқ‘“ to рқ‘”, there is a path рқӣј вҲ¶ рқҗј вҶ’ рқ‘Ң in рқ‘Ң from рқ‘Ұ0 to рқ‘Ұ1 given by рқӣј(рқ‘Ў) = рқҗ»(рқ‘Ҙ0 , рқ‘Ў) such that рқ‘”вҲ— = рқӣј МӮв—Ұрқ‘“вҲ— . рқ‘“вҲ— рқңӢ1 (рқ‘Ӣ, рқ‘Ҙ0 ) рқңӢ1 (рқ‘Ң , рқ‘Ұ0 ) рқӣј МӮ рқ‘”вҲ— рқңӢ1 (рқ‘Ң , рқ‘Ұ1 )

рқ‘”вҲ— ([в„Һ]) = рқӣј МӮ(рқ‘“вҲ— ([в„Һ])).

In other words, we will show that

МӮ([рқ‘“ в—Ұв„Һ]) = [рқӣј]вҲ’1 вҲ— [рқ‘“ в—Ұв„Һ] вҲ— [рқӣј] = [рқӣјвҲ—(рқ‘“ в—Ұв„Һ) вҲ— рқӣј]. [рқ‘”в—Ұв„Һ] = рқӣј

By Lemma 7.3, рқҗ» вҖІ is continuous. Since

рқҗ» вҖІ (рқ‘ , 0) = рқҗ»(в„Һ(рқ‘ ), 1) = рқ‘”(в„Һ(рқ‘ ))

and вҺ§рқӣј(4рқ‘ ) 0 в©Ҫ рқ‘  в©Ҫ 1вҲ•4 вҖІ вҺӘ рқҗ» (рқ‘ , 1) = вҺЁрқҗ»(в„Һ(4рқ‘  вҲ’ 1), 0) 1вҲ•4 в©Ҫ рқ‘  в©Ҫ 1вҲ•2 вҺӘрқӣј(2рқ‘  вҲ’ 1) 1вҲ•2 в©Ҫ рқ‘  в©Ҫ 1 вҺ© вҺ§рқӣј(4рқ‘ ) 0 в©Ҫ рқ‘  в©Ҫ 1вҲ•4 вҺӘ = вҺЁрқ‘“ (в„Һ(4рқ‘  вҲ’ 1)) 1вҲ•4 в©Ҫ рқ‘  в©Ҫ 1вҲ•2 вҺӘрқӣј(2рқ‘  вҲ’ 1) 1вҲ•2 в©Ҫ рқ‘  в©Ҫ 1 вҺ©

for all рқ‘  вҲҲ рқҗј, and рқҗ» вҖІ (0, рқ‘Ў) = рқҗ»(рқ‘Ҙ0 , 1) = рқ‘Ұ1 and рқҗ» вҖІ (1, рқ‘Ў) = рқҗ»(рқ‘Ҙ0 , 1) = рқ‘Ұ1 , it follows that рқҗ» вҖІ is a path homotopy from рқ‘”в—Ұв„Һ to рқӣј вҲ— (рқ‘“ в—Ұв„Һ) вҲ— рқӣј. Thus [рқ‘”в—Ұв„Һ] = [рқӣј вҲ— (рқ‘“ в—Ұв„Һ) вҲ— рқӣј]. This completes the proof.

nodes.title: Theorem 7.38
nodes.originalText:
Let рқ‘Ӣ and рқ‘Ң be topological spaces, and let рқ‘“ вҲ¶ рқ‘Ӣ вҶ’ рқ‘Ң be a homotopy equivalence such that рқ‘“ (рқ‘Ҙ0 ) = рқ‘Ұ0 . Then

рқ‘“вҲ— вҲ¶ рқңӢ1 (рқ‘Ӣ, рқ‘Ҙ0 ) вҶ’ рқңӢ1 (рқ‘Ң , рқ‘Ұ0 )

is an isomorphism.

Proof. Let рқ‘” вҲ¶ рқ‘Ң вҶ’ рқ‘Ӣ be a homotopy inverse of рқ‘“ with рқ‘”(рқ‘Ұ0 ) = рқ‘Ҙ1 , and let рқ‘“ (рқ‘Ҙ1 ) = рқ‘Ұ1 . By assump- tion, we have рқ‘”в—Ұрқ‘“ вүғ idрқ‘Ӣ and рқ‘“ в—Ұрқ‘” вүғ idрқ‘Ң where idрқ‘Ӣ вҲ¶ рқ‘Ӣ вҶ’ рқ‘Ӣ and idрқ‘Ң вҲ¶ рқ‘Ң вҶ’ рқ‘Ң are the identity maps of рқ‘Ӣ and рқ‘Ң , respectively. Thus, by Lemma 7.37, there is a path рқӣј вҲ¶ рқҗј вҶ’ рқ‘Ӣ in рқ‘Ӣ from рқ‘Ҙ0 to рқ‘Ҙ1 and a path рқӣҪ вҲ¶ рқҗј вҶ’ рқ‘Ң from рқ‘Ұ0 to рқ‘Ұ1 such that

(рқ‘”в—Ұ(рқ‘“рқ‘Ҙ0 ))вҲ— = рқ‘”вҲ— в—Ұ(рқ‘“рқ‘Ҙ0 )вҲ— = рқӣј МӮв—Ұ(idрқ‘Ӣ )вҲ— = рқӣј МӮ

and

МӮ рқ‘Ң )вҲ— = рқӣҪМӮ ((рқ‘“рқ‘Ҙ1 )в—Ұрқ‘”)вҲ— = (рқ‘“рқ‘Ҙ1 )вҲ— в—Ұрқ‘”вҲ— = рқӣҪв—Ұ(id

where we have specified the basepoint for the homomorphisms induced by рқ‘“ . In other words, the following two diagrams commute. (idрқ‘Ӣ )вҲ— рқ‘”вҲ— рқңӢ1 (рқ‘Ӣ, рқ‘Ҙ0 ) рқңӢ1 (рқ‘Ӣ, рқ‘Ҙ0 ) рқңӢ1 (рқ‘Ң , рқ‘Ұ0 ) рқңӢ1 (рқ‘Ӣ, рқ‘Ҙ1 ) рқӣј МӮ (рқ‘“рқ‘Ҙ1 )вҲ— рқ‘”вҲ— в—Ұ(рқ‘“рқ‘Ҙ0 )вҲ— рқӣҪМӮ рқңӢ1 (рқ‘Ӣ, рқ‘Ҙ1 ) рқңӢ1 (рқ‘Ң , рқ‘Ұ1 )

Since рқӣјМӮ and рқӣҪМӮ are both isomorphisms, it follows by commutativity of the diagram on the left that рқ‘”вҲ— is an epimorphism, and by commutativity of the diagram on the right that рқ‘”вҲ— is a monomorphism. Hence, рқ‘”вҲ— is an isomorphism. Since (рқ‘“рқ‘Ҙ0 )вҲ— = (рқ‘”вҲ— )вҲ’1 в—ҰМӮ рқӣј it follows that (рқ‘“рқ‘Ҙ0 )вҲ— is an isomorphism.

Chapter 7. The fundamental group 79

We can use Theorem 7.38 to help us determine the fundamental group of certain topological spaces, and as a tool to determine that certain topological spaces are not of the same homotopy type, and hence, not homeo- morphic.

7.4 Exercises

Exercise 7.1 Let рқ‘Ӣ, рқ‘Ң and рқ‘Қ be topological spaces. Show that if рқ‘“ вҲ¶ рқ‘Ӣ вҶ’ рқ‘Ң is homotopic to рқ‘“ вҖІ вҲ¶ рқ‘Ӣ вҶ’ рқ‘Ң and рқ‘” вҲ¶ рқ‘Ң вҶ’ рқ‘Қ is homotopic to рқ‘” вҖІ вҲ¶ рқ‘Ң вҶ’ рқ‘Қ, then рқ‘”в—Ұрқ‘“ вҲ¶ рқ‘Ӣ вҶ’ рқ‘Қ is homotopic to рқ‘” вҖІ в—Ұрқ‘“ вҖІ вҲ¶ рқ‘Ӣ вҶ’ рқ‘Қ.

Exercise 7.2 Let рқ‘Ӣ be a topological space. Show that рқ‘Ӣ is path-connected if and only if every two constant maps рқ‘җ1 вҲ¶ рқ‘Ӣ вҶ’ рқ‘Ӣ and рқ‘җ2 вҲ¶ рқ‘Ӣ вҶ’ рқ‘Ӣ are homotopic.

Exercise 7.3 Let рқ‘Ӣ be a topological space, and let рқ‘Ҙ0 , рқ‘Ҙ1 and рқ‘Ҙ2 be points in рқ‘Ӣ. Show that if рқӣј вҲ¶ рқҗј вҶ’ рқ‘Ӣ is a path in рқ‘Ӣ from рқ‘Ҙ0 to рқ‘Ҙ1 , рқӣҪ вҲ¶ рқҗј вҶ’ рқ‘Ӣ is a path in рқ‘Ӣ from рқ‘Ҙ1 to рқ‘Ҙ2 and рқӣҫ = рқӣј вҲ— рқӣҪ, МӮ рқӣј. then рқӣҫМӮ = рқӣҪв—ҰМӮ

Exercise 7.4 Let рқ‘Ҷ 2 be the 2-sphere considered to be a subspace of в„қ3 with the standard topology. Show that рқңӢ1 (рқ‘Ҷ 2 в§ө {(0, 0, 1)}, рқ‘ ) is the trivial group where рқ‘  = (0, 0, вҲ’1).

Exercise 7.5 Let рқ‘Ӣ be a Hausdorff space, and let рқҗҙ be a subspace of рқ‘Ӣ. Show that if рқҗҙ is a retract of рқ‘Ӣ, then рқҗҙ is a closed subset of рқ‘Ӣ.

Exercise 7.6 Show that the relation of homotopy equivalence is an equivalence relation on any set of topological spaces.

A topological space рқ‘Ӣ is said to be contractible if the identity map of рқ‘Ӣ, idрқ‘Ӣ вҲ¶ рқ‘Ӣ вҶ’ рқ‘Ӣ, is nullho- motopic.

Exercise 7.7 Show that a topological space рқ‘Ӣ is contractible if and only if рқ‘Ӣ has the homotopy type of a point, i.e., a topological space consisting of one point.

Exercise 7.8 Let рқ‘Ӣ be a topological space and let рқ‘Ҷ 1 be the unit circle considered as a subspace of в„қ2 where в„қ2 is given the standard topology. Show that if рқ‘“ вҲ¶ рқ‘Ӣ вҶ’ рқ‘Ҷ 1 is a continuous map that is not surjective, then рқ‘“ is nullhomotopic.

8. The fundamental group of the circle

8.1 Covering spaces The notion of a covering space is a helpful tool for determining fundamental groups. Specifically, we will compute the fundamental group of the circle by first establishing that the map рқ‘қ вҲ¶ в„қ вҶ’ рқ‘Ҷ 1 , given by рқ‘қ(рқ‘Ў) = (cos(2рқңӢрқ‘Ў), sin(2рқңӢрқ‘Ў)),

is a covering map.

nodes.title: Covering spaces
nodes.sectionTitle: The fundamental group of the circle
nodes.originalText:
Let рқҗө and рқҗё be topological spaces. We say that a surjective continuous map рқ‘қ вҲ¶ рқҗё вҶ’ рқҗө is a covering map if for each point рқ‘Ҹ вҲҲ рқҗө there is a neighborhood рқ‘Ҳ such that рқ‘қвҲ’1 (рқ‘Ҳ ) is a disjoint union of open subsets рқ‘үрқңҶ of рқҗё where рқңҶ вҲҲ Оӣ, вЁҶ рқ‘қвҲ’1 (рқ‘Ҳ ) = рқ‘үрқңҶ , рқңҶвҲҲОӣ

and рқ‘қ|рқ‘үрқңҶ вҲ¶ рқ‘үрқңҶ вҶ’ рқ‘Ҳ is a homeomorphism for each рқңҶ вҲҲ Оӣ. We refer to рқҗё as a covering space of рқҗө.

A covering map рқ‘қ вҲ¶ рқҗё вҶ’ рқҗө is sometimes also referred to as a covering projection. Note that рқҗө is often referred to as the base space of the covering map and that рқҗё is often referred to as the total space of the covering map. An open subset рқ‘Ҳ of рқҗө satisfying the condition that вЁҶ рқ‘қвҲ’1 (рқ‘Ҳ ) = рқ‘үрқңҶ , where рқ‘үрқңҶ is an open subset of рқҗё, рқңҶвҲҲОӣ

such that рқ‘қ|рқ‘үрқңҶ вҲ¶ рқ‘үрқңҶ вҶ’ рқ‘Ҳ is a homeomorphism for each рқңҶ вҲҲ Оӣ is said to be evenly covered by рқ‘қ. See Figure 8.1. For each рқ‘Ҹ вҲҲ рқҗө, we often refer to the preimage рқ‘қвҲ’1 (рқ‘Ҹ) of {рқ‘Ҹ} under рқ‘қ as the fiber over рқ‘Ҹ. Note also that the fiber over рқ‘Ҹ is a discrete subspace of рқҗё; each рқ‘үрқңҶ is an open subset of рқҗё and intersects the fiber over рқ‘Ҹ in a single point, and so, this point is open in the fiber over рқ‘Ҹ. Finally, we note that a covering map is a special case of a fiber bundle with discrete fibers.

nodes.title: Example 8.2
nodes.sectionTitle: The fundamental group of the circle
nodes.originalText: Let рқҗө and рқҗё be topological spaces. If рқ‘қ вҲ¶ рқҗё вҶ’ рқҗө is a homeomorphism, then рқ‘қ is also a covering map.

nodes.title: Example 8.3
nodes.sectionTitle: The fundamental group of the circle
nodes.originalText:
Let рқ‘Ӣ be a topological space, and let рқҗ· be a discrete space. Then the projection map onto the first factor, pr 1 вҲ¶ рқ‘Ӣ Г— рқҗ· вҶ’ рқ‘Ӣ, where рқ‘Ӣ Г— рқҗ· is given the product topology, is a covering map. In particular, рқ‘Ӣ is evenly covered by pr 1 .

nodes.title: Local homeomorphisms
nodes.sectionTitle: The fundamental group of the circle
nodes.originalText: Let рқ‘Ӣ and рқ‘Ң be topological spaces. A continuous map рқ‘“ вҲ¶ рқ‘Ӣ вҶ’ рқ‘Ң is said to be a local homeomorphism if for each рқ‘Ҙ вҲҲ рқ‘Ӣ there is a neighborhood рқ‘Ҳ such that рқ‘“ (рқ‘Ҳ ) is open in рқ‘Ң and рқ‘“ |рқ‘Ҳ вҲ¶ рқ‘Ҳ вҶ’ рқ‘“ (рқ‘Ҳ ) is a homeomorphism where рқ‘Ҳ and рқ‘“ (рқ‘Ҳ ) are both given the subspace topology from рқ‘Ӣ and рқ‘Ң , respectively.

nodes.title: Example 8.5
nodes.sectionTitle: The fundamental group of the circle
nodes.originalText:
Let рқ‘Ӣ and рқ‘Ң be topological spaces, and let рқ‘“ вҲ¶ рқ‘Ӣ вҶ’ рқ‘Ң be a homeomorphism. Then рқ‘“ is also a local homeomorphism.

Local homeomorphisms preserve local topological properties such as local connectedness and local path- connectedness. In other words, if рқ‘“ вҲ¶ рқ‘Ӣ вҶ’ рқ‘Ң is a local homeomorphism and, say, рқ‘Ӣ is locally connected then so is рқ‘Ң . A topological space рқ‘Ӣ is locally (path) connected at рқ‘Ҙ вҲҲ рқ‘Ӣ if for each neighborhood рқ‘Ҳ of рқ‘Ҙ there is a (path) connected neighborhood рқ‘ү of рқ‘Ҙ such that рқ‘ү вҠҶ рқ‘Ҳ . We say that рқ‘Ӣ is locally (path) connected if рқ‘Ӣ is locally (path) connected at each рқ‘Ҙ вҲҲ рқ‘Ӣ. Note that a (path) connected space need not be locally (path) connected. Clearly, a covering map is also a local homeomorphism. Thus if рқ‘қ вҲ¶ рқҗё вҶ’ рқҗө is a covering map, then the total space and the base space are locally equivalent but they can differ globally.

The following theorem provides an important example of a covering space that we will use later on to compute the fundamental group of the circle.

nodes.title: Theorem 8.6
nodes.sectionTitle: The fundamental group of the circle
nodes.originalText:
Let в„қ be the set of real numbers equipped with the standard topology, and consider the circle рқ‘Ҷ 1 as a subspace of в„қ2 where в„қ2 is given the standard topology. Then the map рқ‘қ вҲ¶ в„қ вҶ’ рқ‘Ҷ 1 given by рқ‘қ(рқ‘Ў) = (cos(2рқңӢрқ‘Ў), sin(2рқңӢрқ‘Ў)) is a covering map.

82 8.1. Covering spaces

Proof. Clearly, рқ‘қ is continuous and surjective. Let рқ‘Ҳ = рқ‘Ҷ 1 в§ө {(вҲ’1, 0)} and рқ‘ү = рқ‘Ҷ 1 в§ө {(1, 0)}. Then both рқ‘Ҳ and рқ‘ү are open in рқ‘Ҷ 1 , and their union is equal to рқ‘Ҷ 1 . We want to show that they are evenly covered by рқ‘қ. The preimage of рқ‘Ҳ under рқ‘қ is the disjoint union of subsets рқ‘үрқңҶ = (рқңҶ вҲ’ 1вҲ•2, рқңҶ + 1вҲ•2) of в„қ for рқңҶ вҲҲ в„Ө; вЁҶ вЁҶ( 1 1 ) рқ‘қвҲ’1 (рқ‘Ҳ ) = рқ‘үрқңҶ = рқңҶ вҲ’ ,рқңҶ + . рқңҶвҲҲв„Ө рқңҶвҲҲв„Ө 2 2

Similarly, the preimage of рқ‘ү under рқ‘қ is the disjoint union of subsets рқ‘ҠрқңҶ = (рқңҶ, рқңҶ + 1) of в„қ for рқңҶ вҲҲ в„Ө; вЁҶ вЁҶ рқ‘қвҲ’1 (рқ‘ү ) = рқ‘ҠрқңҶ = (рқңҶ, рқңҶ + 1). рқңҶвҲҲв„Ө рқңҶвҲҲв„Ө

Since рқ‘қ is continuous and open, cf. [5, Lemma 14.33], and рқ‘үрқңҶ and рқ‘ҠрқңҶ are open subsets of в„қ, it follows that рқ‘қ|рқ‘үрқңҶ вҲ¶ рқ‘үрқңҶ вҶ’ рқ‘Ҳ and рқ‘қ|рқ‘ҠрқңҶ вҲ¶ рқ‘ҠрқңҶ вҶ’ рқ‘ү are open maps for each рқңҶ вҲҲ в„Ө. Moreover, they are both also bijective for each рқңҶ вҲҲ в„Ө. Hence, by Theorem 5.21 they are both homeomorphisms. Thus рқ‘қ вҲ¶ в„қ вҶ’ рқ‘Ҷ 1 is a covering map.

The covering map рқ‘қ from Theorem 8.6 is illustrated in Figure 8.2. Theorem 8.6 also provides an example of a universal covering space since в„қ is simply connected. It is universal in the following sense: for any covering map рқ‘һ вҲ¶ рқҗё вҶ’ рқ‘Ҷ 1 where we assume that рқҗё is path-connected, there is a covering map рқ‘ҹ вҲ¶ в„қ вҶ’ рқҗё such that рқ‘қ = рқ‘һв—Ұрқ‘ҹ. рқ‘ҹ в„қ рқҗё

then, clearly, вЁҶ( ( )вҲ’1 ) рқ‘қ1 Г— рқ‘қ2 (рқ‘Ҳ1 Г— рқ‘Ҳ2 ) = рқ‘қвҲ’1 (рқ‘Ҳ 1 ) Г— рқ‘қ вҲ’1 (рқ‘Ҳ 2 ) = рқ‘үрқңҶ Г— рқ‘Ҡрқң” . рқңҶвҲҲОӣ рқң”вҲҲО©

Furthermore, рқ‘қ1 Г— рқ‘қ2 |рқ‘үрқңҶ Г—рқ‘Ҡрқң” вҲ¶ рқ‘үрқңҶ Г— рқ‘Ҡрқң” вҶ’ рқ‘Ҳ1 Г— рқ‘Ҳ2 is a homeomorphism for each рқңҶ вҲҲ Оӣ and each рқң” вҲҲ О©. Hence, рқ‘Ҳ1 Г— рқ‘Ҳ2 is a neighborhood of (рқ‘Ҹ1 , рқ‘Ҹ2 ) that is evenly covered by рқ‘қ1 Г— рқ‘қ2 . Thus рқ‘қ1 Г— рқ‘қ2 is a covering map.

8.2 Computing the fundamental group of the circle

In this section we will see how we can use covering spaces as a tool to compute fundamental groups. In particular, we will compute the fundamental group of the circle. By Theorem 8.6, we know that the map рқ‘қ вҲ¶ в„қ вҶ’ рқ‘Ҷ 1 given by рқ‘қ(рқ‘Ў) = (cos(2рқңӢрқ‘Ў), sin(2рқңӢрқ‘Ў)) is a covering map. In order to compute рқңӢ1 (рқ‘Ҷ 1 , рқ‘ 0 ) we will show that the lifting correspondence

ОҰ вҲ¶ рқңӢ1 (рқ‘Ҷ 1 , рқ‘ 0 ) вҶ’ рқ‘қвҲ’1 (рқ‘ 0 ) = в„Ө,

where рқ‘ 0 = (1, 0), is a bijection, and moreover, that it is an isomorphism of groups. This will involve certain lifting theorems. Let рқҗө, рқҗё and рқ‘Ӣ be topological spaces, and let рқ‘қ вҲ¶ рқҗё вҶ’ рқҗө be a continuous map. If we are given a continuous map рқ‘“ вҲ¶ рқ‘Ӣ вҶ’ рқҗө, we are often interested in whether or not there is a continuous map рқ‘“МғвҲ¶ рқ‘Ӣ вҶ’ рқҗё such that рқ‘“ = рқ‘қв—Ұрқ‘“Мғ.

84 8.2. Computing the fundamental group of the circle

This is often referred to as a lifting problem for рқ‘“ .

nodes.title: Liftings
nodes.sectionTitle: The fundamental group of the circle
nodes.originalText:
Let рқҗө, рқҗё and рқ‘Ӣ be topological spaces, and let рқ‘қ вҲ¶ рқҗё вҶ’ рқҗө be a continuous map. A lifting of a continuous map рқ‘“ вҲ¶ рқ‘Ӣ вҶ’ рқҗө is a continuous map рқ‘“МғвҲ¶ рқ‘Ӣ вҶ’ рқҗё such that рқ‘“ = рқ‘қв—Ұрқ‘“Мғ.

We will focus on the case where рқ‘қ is assumed to be a covering map. We will need a theorem that says that any path in the base space of a covering map can be lifted to a path in the total space. In order to prove the theorem we will need the following lemma.

nodes.title: Lebesgue number lemma
nodes.sectionTitle: The fundamental group of the circle
nodes.originalText:
Let (рқ‘Ӣ, рқ‘‘) be a compact metric space, and let оҲӯ be an open cover of рқ‘Ӣ. Then there is a real number рқңҶ > 0 such that for every рқ‘Ҙ вҲҲ рқ‘Ӣ there is a рқ‘Ҳ вҲҲ оҲӯ where рқ–Ў(рқ‘Ҙ; рқңҶ) вҠҶ рқ‘Ҳ .

The number рқңҶ is called a Lebesgue number for the open cover оҲӯ. Note that a Lebesgue number рқңҶ for an open cover оҲӯ is not unique as any positive real number less than рқңҶ is also a Lebesgue number for оҲӯ.

Proof. If рқ‘Ӣ вҲҲ оҲӯ, then every real number рқңҶ > 0 will be a Lebesgue number for оҲӯ. So assume that рқ‘Ӣ is not an element of оҲӯ. By compactness, there is a finite subcollection, say, {рқ‘Ҳ1 , рқ‘Ҳ2 , вҖҰ , рқ‘Ҳрқ‘ӣ } of оҲӯ that covers рқ‘Ӣ. For each рқ‘– вҲҲ {1, 2, вҖҰ , рқ‘ӣ}, let рқ‘үрқ‘– = рқ‘Ӣ в§ө рқ‘Ҳрқ‘– . Then each рқ‘үрқ‘– is non-empty as рқ‘Ҳрқ‘– вү  рқ‘Ӣ for all рқ‘– вҲҲ {1, 2, вҖҰ , рқ‘ӣ}. Let в„қ denote the set of real numbers equipped with the standard topology (and the standard Euclidean metric), and let рқ‘“ вҲ¶ рқ‘Ӣ вҶ’ в„қ be given by рқ‘ӣ 1вҲ‘ рқ‘“ (рқ‘Ҙ) = dist рқ‘үрқ‘– (рқ‘Ҙ) рқ‘ӣ рқ‘–=1

where dist рқ‘үрқ‘– вҲ¶ рқ‘Ӣ вҶ’ в„қ is given by

dist рқ‘үрқ‘– (рқ‘Ҙ) = inf {рқ‘‘(рқ‘Ҙ, рқ‘Ј) вҲЈ рқ‘Ј вҲҲ рқ‘үрқ‘– }.

We claim that dist рқ‘үрқ‘– is continuous for each рқ‘– вҲҲ {1, 2, вҖҰ , рқ‘ӣ}. Let рқ‘Ҙ1 and рқ‘Ҙ2 be two points in рқ‘Ӣ. Then by M3, we have dist рқ‘үрқ‘– (рқ‘Ҙ1 ) в©Ҫ рқ‘‘(рқ‘Ҙ1 , рқ‘Ј) в©Ҫ рқ‘‘(рқ‘Ҙ1 , рқ‘Ҙ2 ) + рқ‘‘(рқ‘Ҙ2 , рқ‘Ј), for all рқ‘Ј вҲҲ рқ‘үрқ‘– , and so, dist рқ‘үрқ‘– (рқ‘Ҙ1 ) вҲ’ рқ‘‘(рқ‘Ҙ1 , рқ‘Ҙ2 ) в©Ҫ dist рқ‘үрқ‘– (рқ‘Ҙ2 ). Similarly, we have dist рқ‘үрқ‘– (рқ‘Ҙ2 ) вҲ’ рқ‘‘(рқ‘Ҙ1 , рқ‘Ҙ2 ) в©Ҫ dist рқ‘үрқ‘– (рқ‘Ҙ1 ). Hence, | dist рқ‘үрқ‘– (рқ‘Ҙ1 ) вҲ’ dist рқ‘үрқ‘– (рқ‘Ҙ2 )| в©Ҫ рқ‘‘(рқ‘Ҙ1 , рқ‘Ҙ2 ). Thus dist рқ‘үрқ‘– is continuous for each рқ‘– вҲҲ {1, 2, вҖҰ , рқ‘ӣ}. Hence, рқ‘“ is also continuous.

Chapter 8. The fundamental group of the circle 85

We claim that рқ‘“ (рқ‘Ҙ) > 0 for all рқ‘Ҙ вҲҲ рқ‘Ӣ. Choose an рқ‘Ҙ вҲҲ рқ‘Ӣ. Since {рқ‘Ҳ1 , рқ‘Ҳ2 , вҖҰ , рқ‘Ҳрқ‘ӣ } covers рқ‘Ӣ, there must be a рқ‘— вҲҲ {1, 2, вҖҰ , рқ‘ӣ} such that рқ‘Ҙ вҲҲ рқ‘Ҳрқ‘— . Since рқ‘Ҳрқ‘— вҲҲ оҲӯ, we know that рқ‘Ҳрқ‘— must be an open set. Thus there is an рқң– > 0 such that рқ–Ў(рқ‘Ҙ; рқң–) вҠҶ рқ‘Ҳрқ‘— , and hence, dist рқ‘үрқ‘— (рқ‘Ҙ) > рқң–. It follows that рқ‘“ (рқ‘Ҙ) > рқң–вҲ•рқ‘ӣ > 0. Thus рқ‘“ (рқ‘Ҙ) > 0 for all рқ‘Ҙ вҲҲ рқ‘Ӣ as claimed. Since рқ‘“ is continuous with a compact domain and рқ‘“ (рқ‘Ҙ) > 0 for all рқ‘Ҙ вҲҲ рқ‘Ӣ, it follows by Theo- rem 6.43 that рқ‘“ must have a minimum value рқңҶ where рқңҶ > 0. We need to show that рқңҶ is a Lebesgue number for {рқ‘Ҳ1 , рқ‘Ҳ2 , вҖҰ , рқ‘Ҳрқ‘ӣ }, and hence, for оҲӯ. Choose an рқ‘Ҙ вҲҲ рқ‘Ӣ. Suppose that there is no рқ‘Ҳрқ‘– вҲҲ {рқ‘Ҳ1 , рқ‘Ҳ2 , вҖҰ , рқ‘Ҳрқ‘ӣ } such that рқ–Ў(рқ‘Ҙ; рқңҶ) вҠҶ рқ‘Ҳрқ‘– . Then dist рқ‘үрқ‘– (рқ‘Ҙ) < рқңҶ for all рқ‘– вҲҲ {1, 2, вҖҰ , рқ‘ӣ}, and thus рқ‘“ (рқ‘Ҙ) < рқңҶ. This contradicts the fact that рқңҶ is the minimum value of рқ‘“ over рқ‘Ӣ. Hence, there must be a рқ‘Ҳрқ‘– вҲҲ оҲӯ such that рқ–Ў(рқ‘Ҙ; рқңҶ) вҠҶ рқ‘Ҳрқ‘– . Thus рқңҶ is a Lebesgue number as claimed.

Then clearly, рқҗј = рқҗҙ вҲӘ рқҗ· and рқҗҙ вҲ© рқҗ· = вҲ…. Thus for all рқ‘Ў вҲҲ оүҒ , we have of рқ‘  and рқ‘қв—Ұрқ‘“Мғ1 (рқ‘Ў) = рқ‘“ (рқ‘Ў) = рқ‘қв—Ұрқ‘“Мғ2 (рқ‘Ў) implying that рқ‘“Мғ1 (рқ‘Ў) = рқ‘“Мғ2 (рқ‘Ў) as рқ‘қ|рқ‘ү вҲ¶ рқ‘үрқң” вҶ’ рқ‘Ҳ is a homeomorphism. рқң” Hence, рқ‘Ў вҲҲ рқҗҙ and оүҒ вҠҶ рқҗҙ. Thus рқҗҙ is open. Finally, we need to show that рқҗ· is open. Choose an рқ‘  вҲҲ рқҗ·. Let рқ‘Ҳ be a neighborhood of рқ‘“ (рқ‘ ) that is evenly covered by рқ‘қ. Proof. We prove the statement about path homotopies. The proof of the first part of the theorem is similar to the proof of Theorem 8.12, where we make use of Lemma 8.10 to break рқҗј Г— рқҗј into smaller squares [рқ‘ рқ‘–вҲ’1 , рқ‘ рқ‘– ] Г— [рқ‘Ўрқ‘–вҲ’1 , рқ‘Ўрқ‘– ] that are mapped by рқҗ» into open sets of рқҗө that are evenly covered by рқ‘қ. See [4, pp. 343вҖ“344] for details. Assume that рқҗ» is a path homotopy from the path рқ‘“0 вҲ¶ рқҗј вҶ’ рқҗө to the path рқ‘“1 вҲ¶ рқҗј вҶ’ рқҗө where рқ‘“0 (0) = рқ‘“1 (0) = рқ‘Ҹ0 and рқ‘“0 (1) = рқ‘“1 (1) = рқ‘Ҹ1 . Thus by uniqueness рқҗ» Мғ 0 = рқ‘җрқ‘’ . In other words, Мғ рқҗ»(0, рқ‘Ў) = рқ‘’0 for all рқ‘Ў вҲҲ рқҗј. Similarly, let рқҗ»1 вҲ¶ рқҗј вҶ’ рқҗө be the map given by
